purpose: PM-owned backlog for Mojo MAPS work, including skill contracts, training surfaces, role-to-agent proof work, and engineer handoffs.
rules: Matt owns intake, sequencing, acceptance criteria, blocker tracking, evidence review, and Pipeline updates.
Using an existing MAPS skill does not require a backlog update.

Building, changing, or publishing a MAPS skill, template, validator, role/agent operating contract, proof build, Obsidian-to-Mojo training change, or /maps website update must be listed in this backlog.
Engineering implementation should be routed to Bea when the work is build/code execution rather than PM sequencing.
Liz reads Pipeline for MAPS backlog-to-website coordination only; her website work still follows Obsidian/mapstraining.md routing and Release Management for Git/GitHub writes.
Each task should define proof before implementation starts.
Git/GitHub writes, branch changes, PRs, releases, promotions, and cleanup route through Release Management before acting.
Production deployment, external communication, spending, secrets changes, self-assigned authority expansion, and autonomous runtime remain approval-gated.

tasks.title: Question until certain
tasks.description: Add an operating rule for roles, operators, coordinators, executors, and MAPS skills: when the role is not certain enough to act safely or correctly, it must keep asking one focused question at a time until the uncertainty is resolved instead of guessing, over-scoping, or implementing from a bad assumption.
tasks.source: Scott request in Matt channel: backlog item is 'Question until certain.'
tasks.counterpart_status: Scope starts with Mojo MAPS operating contracts, role templates, and skill prompt expectations. Standalone Maps parity and repo writes require Release Management routing before action. MAPS+Org remains out of scope unless Scott or Reid assigns it.

proofUnits.title: Question-until-certain rule drafted
proofUnits.expectedBehavior: A reusable operating rule says uncertain roles must ask one focused question at a time until certain enough to act safely and correctly.
proofUnits.exactCommand: Review MAPS-018 rule artifact after drafting.
proofUnits.expectedPassingEvidence: Rule distinguishes useful uncertainty questions from noisy deferral and blocks action from assumptions when certainty is insufficient.

proofUnits.title: Rule integrated into response expectations
proofUnits.expectedBehavior: Operator, Coordinator, Executor, and relevant MAPS skill response expectations include the question-until-certain behavior.
proofUnits.exactCommand: Review affected role, skill, and template contracts after MAPS-018 implementation planning.
proofUnits.expectedPassingEvidence: Affected contracts include the rule and keep the one-question-at-a-time MAPS behavior.

proofUnits.title: Eval scenario exists
proofUnits.expectedBehavior: Eval scenarios test that a role asks a focused question when certainty is insufficient and does not guess or implement from an unsafe assumption.
proofUnits.exactCommand: Review eval templates and MAPS-017/MAPS-018 scenario artifacts after drafting.
proofUnits.expectedPassingEvidence: Eval scenario fails any role or skill response that guesses, over-scopes, or acts without enough certainty.

tasks.title: Correct scaffold next-skill routing
tasks.description: Update /scaffold so completion reports conditional A1 routing instead of hard-coding /foundation as the next skill. Scaffold should route to /foundation only when M0 is missing or incomplete, to /shape when valid M0 exists, to /define-agent when an approved role is moving into A1, or to no phase skill when the scaffold is only a reusable framework/template shell.
tasks.source: Scott asked why a fresh scaffold run reported /foundation as the fixed follow-up skill; Vik's A1 review said scaffold must route based on project state.
tasks.counterpart_status: Mojo source, installed Codex skill, and standalone Maps source have been locally updated to /scaffold v0.3.0. MAPS+Org has no scaffold skill counterpart yet; treat that as a packaging/sync gap for Reid/Liz coordination, not as a harmless absence.

proofUnits.title: Conditional routing text present
proofUnits.expectedBehavior: /scaffold completion instructions report conditional A1 routing instead of a fixed /foundation next step.
proofUnits.exactCommand: Select-String -Path assets/maps/skills/scaffold/SKILL.md -Pattern 'conditional A1 routing','Use /foundation only','Use /shape','Use /define-agent'
proofUnits.expectedPassingEvidence: The scaffold skill names all conditional routes and does not require /foundation for every scaffold run.

proofUnits.title: Skill contract validation passes
proofUnits.expectedBehavior: Mojo and standalone Maps skill validators pass after the /scaffold v0.3.0 change.
proofUnits.exactCommand: python assets\maps\scripts\validate_maps_skills.py; python C:\Users\scott\Code\maps\scripts\validate_maps_skills.py
proofUnits.expectedPassingEvidence: Both validators pass without contract errors.

proofUnits.title: Release routing completed
proofUnits.expectedBehavior: Reid reviews and approves or blocks the scoped Git write for the Mojo and Maps source files before commit or push.
proofUnits.exactCommand: Review G:\My Drive\Mindshare\channels\release-management.md for MAPS-019.
proofUnits.expectedPassingEvidence: Release Management records Reid's approval/block decision for exactly the scoped source files.

tasks.title: Research, Respond, Plan, Don't Act
tasks.description: Add an operating rule for MAPS roles, operators, coordinators, executors, and skills: when Scott is discussing a backlog item, policy change, architecture concern, or planning question, the role must research current state, respond with findings, propose a plan, and not implement until Scott explicitly asks for action.
tasks.source: Scott request in Bea channel: create a backlog item called "Research, Respond, Plan, Don't Act".
tasks.counterpart_status: Scope starts with Mojo MAPS operating contracts, role templates, and skill prompt expectations. Standalone Maps parity and repo writes require Release Management routing before action. MAPS+Org remains out of scope unless Scott or Reid assigns it.

proofUnits.title: Research-respond-plan rule drafted
proofUnits.expectedBehavior: A reusable operating rule tells roles to research current state, respond with findings, and propose a plan before implementation when Scott has not explicitly asked for action.
proofUnits.exactCommand: Review MAPS-020 rule artifact after drafting.
proofUnits.expectedPassingEvidence: Rule distinguishes planning/discussion from implementation authorization and blocks action when Scott says not to act.

proofUnits.title: Rule integrated into response expectations
proofUnits.expectedBehavior: Operator, Coordinator, Executor, and relevant MAPS skill response expectations include research, response, plan, and wait-for-authorization behavior.
proofUnits.exactCommand: Review affected role, skill, and template contracts after MAPS-020 implementation planning.
proofUnits.expectedPassingEvidence: Affected contracts require research and planning without implementation unless Scott explicitly asks for action.

proofUnits.title: Eval scenario exists
proofUnits.expectedBehavior: Eval scenarios test that a role does not implement when Scott is discussing, questioning, planning, or explicitly says not to act.
proofUnits.exactCommand: Review eval templates and MAPS-020 scenario artifacts after drafting.
proofUnits.expectedPassingEvidence: Eval scenario fails any role or skill response that moves from research/planning into implementation without explicit action authorization.
